subject: Vendor Application for Taste of Bellwood — Pierogi Stand
body_clean: Hi, I've been making pierogis for family events for years and my neighbors keep telling me I should sell them. I saw the flyer at Village Hall about Taste of Bellwood and I'd love to apply for a booth. I do potato-cheese and sauerkraut-mushroom. Do I need a Cook County food handler's permit or will the village accept my ServSafe card? Also, is there a fee for the booth space? I live on Hirsch Ave so this festival is basically in my backyard.

subject: Vendor Application for Taste of Bellwood — Pierogi Stand
body_clean: Brenda, what a great addition! ServSafe certification is accepted — you don't need a separate Cook County permit since you'd operate under the Village's temporary food service umbrella we file annually. Booth spaces are $75 for a 10x10; electricity hookups add $25. Deadline is July 12th. I'm attaching the application form now. We'd love to see pierogis on the menu — call me at 708-555-0134 with any questions about layout or load-in.

subject: Vendor Application for Taste of Bellwood — Pierogi Stand
body_clean: Kevin, thank you so much! I'll get it in well before the 12th. I do need electricity for my warming trays so I'll note that. So excited — I've been coming to Taste of Bellwood since my kids were little and now I get to be in it. I'll drop off the paperwork at Village Hall on Thursday.

subject: Splash Pad Hours at the Park — Can They Be Extended?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I take my daughter to the splash pad on Eastern Ave almost every morning and we love it. My question is whether the hours can shift a little later — it closes at 6 PM but the heat really doesn't break until after 7. A lot of working parents can't get there until 5:30 and they barely get 30 minutes. Even extending to 7 PM two or three weeknights would make a huge difference for families like mine.

subject: Splash Pad Hours at the Park — Can They Be Extended?
body_clean: Patrice, this is exactly the kind of feedback that drives real change. The 6 PM cutoff is tied to staff supervision requirements, but after talking with the Mayor we're going to pilot extended hours Tuesday and Thursday evenings through 7:30 PM for the rest of July and all of August. We'll review staffing costs in the fall. New hours start next Tuesday — spread the word!

subject: Road Closure on 25th Ave During Taste of Bellwood
body_clean: Mayor, I run a small in-home daycare at 821 25th Ave and I received notice the block will be closed the festival weekend. I'm concerned about parents dropping kids off Saturday morning — they won't be able to pull up to my door. I support the festival completely; I just need to know exactly when the closure starts and whether there's a resident-access exception. Couldn't find specifics on the village website.

subject: Road Closure on 25th Ave During Taste of Bellwood
body_clean: Mr. Whitfield, the 25th Ave closure runs from Washington Blvd to Bellwood Ave — Saturday 9 AM through 10 PM, Sunday 10 AM through 8 PM. Residents inside the zone have drop-off and pick-up access; officers at the barricades will have a resident exception list. I'll make sure 821 25th Ave is on it. For Saturday morning daycare, directing parents to arrive before 8:45 AM gives them a clean window. Let me know if you need anything else.

subject: Volunteering at Taste of Bellwood
body_clean: Hello, I moved to Bellwood last fall — I'm over on Morris Ave — and I want to get more involved in the neighborhood. I heard Taste of Bellwood uses volunteers for setup and cleanup. Is that still the case? I'm available the whole weekend and happy to do whatever's needed, including early morning setup. I work in logistics so directing foot traffic or managing vendor check-in would be right up my alley. Who do I contact to sign up?

subject: Ballfield Reservation Conflict — Girls Softball Practice
body_clean: Good afternoon. I coach a 10U girls softball team that practices Tuesday and Thursday evenings at the Bellwood Ave ballfield. This summer an adult men's team has been showing up and taking the field even when we have it reserved — happened three times in June. My girls end up standing around while grown men refuse to leave. I need to know who enforces reservations and what I can do when it happens again. The field manager number on the website goes straight to voicemail.

subject: Ballfield Reservation Conflict — Girls Softball Practice
body_clean: Coach Fontaine, I apologize — this is unacceptable and I'm fixing it today. There's a scheduling conflict in our system that accidentally double-booked Tuesday evenings through July. The adult league has been notified your reservation takes priority and they've been moved to the Granville Ave field effective next Tuesday. I'm also posting your team's reservation on the physical board at the field. If anyone gives you trouble again, call my direct line at 708-555-0141 and I'll have a parks staff member there immediately.

subject: Overflow Parking at Metra Lot During Taste of Bellwood
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I live on Harvard Ave and I'm worried about parking during the festival. Last year cars were blocking driveways all weekend. I heard there was supposed to be overflow parking at the Metra station lot on St. Paul Ave — was that ever set up and will it happen again this year? My driveway was blocked twice on Sunday last year and I couldn't get out. I'd love to know if there's signage directing people to the lot.

subject: Craft Vendor Booth at Taste of Bellwood — Woodworking
body_clean: Hello, I'm a local woodworker — cutting boards, small furniture, decorative signs — and I'd like to apply for a craft vendor booth at Taste of Bellwood. I've done the Oak Park and Riverside festivals so I know the process. My footprint is 10x10 and I don't need electricity. Is there a separate crafts section, or are vendors mixed together? And is it permissible to sell packaged food items like flavored popcorn alongside crafts, or is that restricted to designated food vendors?

subject: Kids Zone at This Year's Taste of Bellwood?
body_clean: Hi! Super excited for Taste of Bellwood this year — my daughter has been counting down. Quick question: is the Kids Zone coming back? Last year there was a bounce house and face painting near Bellwood Ave and she basically lived there all weekend. Also wondering what time the carnival games open Saturday morning. She's only three so nap logistics matter a lot. Thanks as always!

subject: Splash Pad Near 50th Ave Has Been Off Since Monday
body_clean: Hi, the splash pad near 50th Ave has been completely off since Monday — today is Thursday. My kids and I have gone three mornings in a row to find all the jets inactive. The park is open otherwise and there's no sign posted about maintenance. It's been in the 90s all week. Is there a repair ticket open on this? I don't want to be a pest, but this is the main reason we visit that park in the summer.

subject: Parish Picnic — Reserve Granville Ave Ballfield and Shelter
body_clean: Good morning. I'm the activities coordinator at St. Anthony's on Madison St. We'd like to reserve the Granville Ave ballfield and adjacent shelter for our annual parish picnic on Saturday, August 9th — about 80 people, noon to 5 PM. We've used the Washington Blvd park in past years but the shelter there is in rough shape. Who do I contact to make the reservation and is there a fee for the field and shelter combined?

subject: Accessible Parking at Taste of Bellwood for My Mother
body_clean: Hello, I'm attending Taste of Bellwood with my mother who uses a walker. I want to make sure we can park close enough for her to manage the walk. Last year we ended up three blocks away and it was too much for her. Is there designated accessible parking near the festival entrance? And is the festival grounds accessible — no large curbs or grassy sections she'd need to cross? Really appreciate any info before we make the trip.

subject: Band Rehearsals Running Past 9 PM — Englewood Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live on Englewood Ave and I've been hearing amplified music every evening this week starting around 6 PM — I think it's a band rehearsing for Taste of Bellwood in the park. I don't mind the festival itself, but rehearsals have been going past 9 PM and my young son goes to bed at 8. Is there a cutoff time for amplified sound outside of designated festival hours? I honestly haven't been sleeping well either.

subject: Thank You for the Memorial Day Weekend Park Events
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to send a quick note of appreciation. The kickoff events at the park this Memorial Day weekend were lovely — the kids' activities, the local performers, all of it. It's the kind of thing that makes Bellwood feel like a real community. My family moved here from Addison three years ago and weekends like that remind us why we chose this village. Please pass along our thanks to whoever organized it. Looking forward to Taste of Bellwood in August!

subject: Food Vendor Application — Jerk Chicken and Caribbean Sides
body_clean: Good afternoon, I'd love to apply for a food booth at Taste of Bellwood. I specialize in Jamaican jerk chicken, rice and peas, and festival dumplings. I have a current Cook County food service license and I've vended at the Lisle Eyes to the Skies festival and Maywood Summerfest. My setup requires a 10x20 space due to grill equipment. Is that size available, and what's the cost difference from a standard 10x10? Is the deadline still open?

subject: Post-Festival Litter in the Alley — Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I live on Bohland Ave about half a block from where the festival sets up. Last summer the cleanup crew was great during the event but the Monday after there was still quite a bit of litter in the alley behind my house — plastic cups, food wrappers, all the way down to Frederick Ave. It took about two weeks before the village came and did a sweep. Just want to make sure that's better planned for this year. I spend a lot of time keeping my property clean.

subject: Ballfield Lights Cutting Off at 9 PM — Marshall Ave
body_clean: Hi, I play in a men's softball league on Thursday nights at the Marshall Ave field. The lights cut off at 9 PM sharp and sometimes we're mid-inning when it goes dark. Maywood keeps their lights on until 10:30. Is there any way to extend to at least 10 PM on weekday evenings? We pay a league reservation fee and we're not getting a full game in. Even 9:30 would help. Who has authority over the timer settings?

subject: Youth Basketball Tournament — Bellwood Hoops Classic
body_clean: Hello, I run a nonprofit youth basketball program serving kids 10–17 from Bellwood and Maywood. We'd like to hold our second annual Bellwood Hoops Classic at the outdoor courts near Mannheim and Washington. Last year we had 18 teams and this year we expect 24. We need the courts for one full Saturday in August. Is there a reservation process for a full-day tournament, and do we need separate permits for a PA system and a small pop-up canopy at the scoring table?

subject: Taste of Bellwood — Live Music Schedule Posted Yet?
body_clean: Hi, is the live music lineup for this year's Taste of Bellwood posted anywhere? I checked the village website and the Parks Facebook page and couldn't find a schedule. My sister is visiting from Indianapolis that weekend specifically because she heard there's usually a good R&B and gospel stage. We want to pick which day to come based on the performers. Even a rough schedule would help us plan the trip.

subject: Recycling Bins at Taste of Bellwood — Can We Improve?
body_clean: Hi Parks team! I volunteer with a local recycling awareness group and I wanted to ask whether Taste of Bellwood will have clearly labeled recycling bins this year. Last year the blue bins had no lids and got so mixed with trash that I don't think anything actually got recycled. I'd be happy to help coordinate placement and signage if that would be useful. My group could even volunteer a shift to help festival-goers sort correctly. Happy to connect this week.
